topic: The Stanford Prison Experiment and the psychology of power

script.title: The Experiment That Proved Power Corrupts Anyone

scenes.narration: In 1971, ordinary college students were handed absolute power over other students. Within six days, the experiment had to be shut down. What happened next says something dark about you.
scenes.image_prompt: Empty prison corridor in black and white film noir style, harsh shadows falling through iron bars, single overhead light swinging, cinematic high contrast, no people

scenes.narration: The psychologist Philip Zimbardo assigned them roles at random. Half became guards. Half became prisoners. Nothing else was changed. The outcome was not supposed to matter.
scenes.image_prompt: Two identical uniforms hanging on hooks in black and white, one dark and one light, film noir lighting, shadows creeping across concrete wall, no people

scenes.narration: Within forty-eight hours, the guards began humiliating the prisoners. They stripped them, isolated them, invented punishments. No one told them to be cruel. The uniform was enough.
scenes.image_prompt: A wooden baton resting on a metal desk in black and white film noir, harsh shadows, dust particles in a single beam of light, no people

scenes.narration: The prisoners broke down faster. One had a breakdown within thirty-six hours. Others became silent, obedient, hollow. They forgot they could simply walk out the door.
scenes.image_prompt: An empty chair in the center of a dim cell, black and white film noir, light bleeding through barred window, heavy shadows, no people

scenes.narration: This was not a study about bad people. Every participant had been screened as psychologically healthy. The role itself rewrote who they were.
scenes.image_prompt: A cracked mirror on a concrete wall in black and white film noir, reflecting only darkness, deep shadows, cinematic contrast, no people

scenes.narration: Zimbardo himself got lost in it. He played the prison warden and stopped seeing the harm. It took an outside observer to remind him these were human beings.
scenes.image_prompt: A clipboard abandoned on a concrete floor in black and white film noir, long shadows stretching across the frame, single bare bulb above, no people

scenes.narration: The lesson most people miss is not that power corrupts. It is that identity is thinner than you think. A uniform, a title, a crowd, and you become someone else.
scenes.image_prompt: A uniform jacket hanging on a lone chair in black and white film noir, dramatic shadow on concrete wall, atmospheric lighting, no people

scenes.narration: You like to believe you would have been the one to speak up. Statistically, you would not. The most uncomfortable truth is that almost no one does.
scenes.image_prompt: A heavy steel door standing slightly open in black and white film noir, darkness beyond, long shadow cast toward the viewer, no people
